title: triticum vulgare var. turgidum
intro: Triticum vulgare var. turgidum, commonly known as spelt, is a type of ancient grain that belongs to the wheat family and is closely related to durum wheat and emmer. The plant has been valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. Spelt has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing essential nutrients such as iron, zinc, and B vitamins. Herbal preparations of spelt, including tea, flour, and supplements, can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
